title: Dryopteris effusa
intro: Dryopteris effusa is a species of fern, belonging to the genus Dryopteris, commonly known as the male fern or buckler fern. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris effusa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include tinctures, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including parasitic infections and fever.
taxonomy: Dryopteris effusa is classified as a species of fern within the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, commonly known as the wood ferns. The species is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world, including parts of Asia, Africa, and the Pacific Islands. Dryopteris effusa is a terrestrial fern, typically growing in shaded, humid environments with rich soil. Its classification also places it within the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all ferns.
